brands.name: That 1 Painter
brands.notes: Fast-growing residential/commercial painting franchise under ResiBrands; began franchising in 2021-2022 and grew from zero to 265+ territories (94 franchisees) by end of 2024. Strong Item 19 average gross revenue but a very young system with rapid unit expansion outpacing operating history.

riskFlags.title: Very new, rapidly expanding system
riskFlags.detail: Began franchising in 2021-2022; the system added 203 franchised territories in a single year, ending 2024 with 94 franchisees in 265 territories plus 117 signed-but-unopened agreements. Explosive growth with limited multi-year operating history means few units have proven long-term durability.

riskFlags.title: Item 19 average skewed by top performers
riskFlags.detail: Reported ~$843K-$894K average gross revenue is computed across a small base of locations open 12+ months and is heavily influenced by top-third performers (franchisor markets a top-third average of ~$1.65M). Averages should not be read as typical unit performance for a system this young.

riskFlags.title: Above-category advertising obligations
riskFlags.detail: Beyond the 6% royalty and 2% brand fund, franchisees must spend $3,000/month on local advertising in year one, then the greater of 4% of monthly gross or $3,000/month, plus a $650-$1,300/month ad management fee and a ~$461/territory/week minimum royalty. Total ongoing fee load is meaningful for an emerging brand.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Franchisor site lists $96,500-$137,000; FDD-derived summaries cite $113K-$189K depending on operating model (subcontractor vs employee). Range varies by model.

totalInvestmentMax.note: 2024 FDD total investment $139,750-$189,000; franchisor site shows lower $96,500-$137,000 for one model.

adFundPercent.note: 2% brand fund (capped at 3%); separate local advertising minimum of $3,000/month or 4% of gross applies.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Item 19: $843,538 average gross revenue across 19 franchised/affiliate locations open 12+ months as of 12/31/2023. Represents gross revenue, not profit; skewed by top performers.

totalUnits.note: 94 franchisees operating 265 territories + 1 company-owned business operating 6 territories as of 12/31/2024. Counted by operators; territory count is far higher (265+).

yearFranchising.note: Began offering franchises June 2021 (some FDD summaries cite first units in 2022).

sbaApproved.note: Listed on the SBA Franchise Directory per franchisor site.

isGrowing.note: Jumped from #443 to #230 on Entrepreneur Franchise 500 after adding ~179 units in a year.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Multi-territory ownership offered; system structured around territories.

brands.name: Lawn Pride
brands.notes: Lawn fertilization/weed-control franchise owned by Neighborly (acquired 2022); began franchising January 2023 and grew roughly 6x to 36 units by end of 2024. Very young franchise system with thin franchisee-level performance data (Item 19 reflects only the original affiliate location).

riskFlags.title: Brand-new franchise system with minimal franchisee track record
riskFlags.detail: Began franchising January 2023 with zero franchised units through 2022. By end of 2024 there were 36 businesses (35 franchised + 1 affiliate). Almost no franchised units have multiple years of operating history, so performance and unit-survival data are not yet meaningful.

riskFlags.title: Item 19 covers only the affiliate location, not franchisees
riskFlags.detail: The 2023 FDD Item 19 reported per-invoice averages ($81.53 residential / $258.48 commercial in 2022) for the single affiliate-owned Indianapolis business only. There is no franchisee-level revenue disclosure, so prospective buyers have no system-wide earnings benchmark.

riskFlags.title: Royalty at/above lawn-care category norm
riskFlags.detail: Up to 8% royalty plus 2% marketing fee is at the higher end versus several established lawn-care franchises, a consideration for a system without proven franchisee margins.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum liquid capital; minimum net worth $250,000.

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2023 FDD: $134,770-$153,420. Later third-party summaries cite a wider $141K-$244K range.

totalInvestmentMax.note: 2023 FDD upper bound; some 2025/2026 third-party sources report up to $243,710.

franchiseFee.note: 2023 FDD lists $40,000; later summaries cite $40,050-$62,300 reflecting multi-territory pricing.

royaltyPercent.note: Up to 8% of gross sales, with minimum fees applicable.

totalUnits.note: 36 businesses as of 12/31/2024 (35 franchised + 1 affiliate-operated). Added ~30 units in 2024.

yearFounded.note: Original Indianapolis affiliate operation dates to 1993.

trainingWeeks.note: Roughly two weeks of initial training at headquarters.

item19Available.note: Item 19 present but limited to per-invoice averages for the single affiliate location.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Multi-territory ownership offered via Neighborly platform.

ownerTypes.note: Neighborly markets manager-run/semi-absentee paths; confirm in current FDD.

brands.name: True North Restoration
brands.notes: Water/fire/mold property-damage restoration franchise that consolidated several independent companies under one brand circa 2021 and began franchising in 2021. Emerging, low-cost-entry system (~10-18 units) with an Item 19 gross-sales figure; founding-year sourcing conflicts between an operator legacy date (1984) and brand formation (2019-2021).

riskFlags.title: Emerging system, small unit base
riskFlags.detail: Brand formed when ~six independent restoration companies combined (circa 2019-2021) and began franchising in 2021. Unit counts reported across sources are small and inconsistent (FDD ~8-10 franchised + company units; one directory cites ~18 in 2024). Limited history means franchisee outcomes are not yet well established.

riskFlags.title: Conflicting founding-year claims across sources
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz lists founding year 1984 (likely a legacy operator date), while news/brand sources describe the True North brand forming around 2019 with six companies joining in late 2021. Treat the brand as effectively new despite older operator roots.

riskFlags.title: Royalty/fee figures vary between sources
riskFlags.detail: Reported royalties range from 5% (Vetted Biz) to 7% (multiple aggregators) of gross revenue, with a 2% marketing fee; franchise fee reported as $25,000-$49,500. Discrepancies suggest FDD-version drift; verify against the current FDD before relying on a specific figure.

liquidCapitalMin.note: ~$80,000 liquid capital and ~$350,000 net worth cited by aggregators/franchisor.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Vetted Biz cites $87,300-$195,000; other sources cite $139,530-$233,300 depending on territory size. Ranges conflict across FDD versions.

totalInvestmentMax.note: Upper bound varies ($195K to $233,300) by source/territory.

franchiseFee.note: Reported as $25,000-$49,500; $49,500 is the commonly cited full-territory fee.

royaltyPercent.note: Multiple aggregators cite 7% of gross revenue; Vetted Biz cites 5%. Conflict noted in risk flags.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Reported Item 19 average yearly gross sales of $654,369. Small sample for an emerging system; represents gross sales not profit. Verify in current FDD.

totalUnits.note: FDD-era ~10 U.S. units; one 2024 directory reports ~18 with 125% 3-yr growth. Sources conflict.

isGrowing.note: Named a fastest-growing emerging franchise for 2026; ranked #461 Franchise 500 in 2025.

brands.name: Fundraising University
brands.notes: School-sports fundraising franchise (founded ~2009-2011, Gilbert AZ) helping high school teams run fundraising campaigns; ~28 units in 2022 growing to ~58 in 2025. Item 19 shows a high system average gross sales but with wide dispersion across thirds; royalty/fee structure is variable and tiered.

riskFlags.title: Wide performance dispersion in Item 19
riskFlags.detail: 2022 Item 19 average gross sales of $648,427 masks a large spread: upper third averaged $1,203,691, middle third $487,628, and lower third only $253,962. The system average is pulled up by top performers; bottom-third results are roughly a fifth of the top third.

riskFlags.title: Complex, variable fee structure
riskFlags.detail: Royalty, brand-fund and local-marketing obligations are not flat percentages; they vary by length of operation, number of territories, and the number of high-school-age students in a territory. Some sources also reference a tiered monthly royalty ($1,500 rising to $2,000). Buyers must model fees against their specific territory.

riskFlags.title: Franchise-fee figures conflict across sources/FDD versions
riskFlags.detail: Reported initial franchise fee ranges from $59,500 to $69,500 to $80,000 depending on source and territory size (student-population tiers). The 2024 FDD review cites $69,500; later listings cite $60,000-$80,000. Confirm against the current FDD.

liquidCapitalMin.note: ~$50,000 liquid capital; net worth requirement reported ~$115,000.

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2023 FDD: $89,850-$101,501. Older 2023 reviews cite a lower $77K-$85K range from an earlier FDD.

franchiseFee.note: 2023 FDD review cites $69,500; other sources cite $59,500-$80,000 by territory size. Conflict noted in risk flags.

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2022 Item 19 average gross sales of $648,427 across performing outlets. Represents gross sales (all consideration from operations), not profit; wide dispersion across thirds.

totalUnits.note: ~58 total units in 2025 (51 franchised + 7 company-owned); 2022 FDD showed 28 (23 franchised + 5 affiliate).

yearFounded.note: Founded ~2009 by Mike Bahun; the franchisor site '15 years' badge implies ~2011. Sources differ slightly.

trainingWeeks.note: Initial training included ($15,000 training fee in startup costs); duration not precisely disclosed in summary.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Territories defined by high-school-age student population.

canRunFromHome.note: Sales/service model that can be run from a home office.

brands.name: Rumble Boxing
brands.notes: Boxing-themed boutique fitness franchise; grew from 13 to 84 franchised studios (2022-2024). Formerly an Xponential Fitness brand, acquired by Extraordinary Brands in 2025. Significant inherited regulatory/legal baggage from Xponential, high capital requirements, and only 38% of qualified studios meeting the Item 19 average revenue.

riskFlags.title: Xponential Fitness regulatory and class-action settlements
riskFlags.detail: Rumble's former parent Xponential Fitness agreed to pay nearly $40M to settle with the FTC (~$17M) and 500+ current/former franchisees over allegations it violated the FTC Act and Franchise Rule by misleading prospective franchisees about studio opening timelines. NY AG separately secured $3.9M+ for misleading franchise owners. Shareholder class actions alleged fraud.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Times / Athletech News / NY Attorney General

riskFlags.title: Studio closures across the former parent's system
riskFlags.detail: Litigation alleged Xponential falsely claimed it had never closed a store while reportedly closing at least 30 studios; the company closed 140 total units in 2025 against 341 gross openings. Rumble's company-owned count fell from 5 (2023) to 1 (2024). Boutique-fitness closure risk is elevated.

riskFlags.title: Ownership change / leadership churn
riskFlags.detail: Extraordinary Brands acquired Rumble Boxing (and CycleBar) from Xponential in 2025, and Xponential installed a new CEO amid slowing sales. New ownership and management of the brand introduces transition risk for franchisees' support and standards.

riskFlags.title: Most studios fall short of the average revenue
riskFlags.detail: Per the 2025 FDD Item 19, only 38% of qualified studios met or exceeded the $492,590 average gross revenue (median $399,647). Average monthly membership attrition was 8.8%. High investment ($510K-$1.14M) against sub-average performance for the majority is a meaningful caution.

adFundPercent.note: 2% brand development fund; plus local advertising of greater of $1,500 or 2% of prior month's gross sales.

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2025 FDD Item 19: 56 qualified studios (operated full year) averaged $492,590 gross revenue, median $399,647; only 38% met/exceeded the average. Gross revenue, not profit.

totalUnits.note: 84 franchised + 1 company-owned as of end of 2024.

yearFounded.note: Founded January 2017 in Chelsea, NYC as a boxing-inspired group fitness concept.

yearFranchising.note: Began franchising under Xponential circa 2018; exact first-franchise year should be confirmed in FDD.

isGrowing.note: Ranked #487 on Entrepreneur 2026 Franchise 500; franchised unit count still rising though company-owned shrank and parent-system closures occurred.

minFootprint.note: Boutique studio footprint typically ~1,500-2,500 sq ft; confirm in current FDD.
